title: AWS access key id
fixHint: Rotate the AWS key in IAM and remove it from source.

title: GitHub token
fixHint: Revoke the token at github.com/settings/tokens and remove from source.

title: Shell command built from interpolation/concatenation
fixHint: Command injection risk. Use execFile/spawn with an argument array; never interpolate user input into a shell string.

title: Malformed JSON: data-broken.json
fixHint: Fix the JSON syntax error at the reported location.

title: Database connection string with inline credentials
fixHint: Move the DB URL (with its embedded user:pass) to an env var; rotate the password.

title: TLS certificate verification disabled
fixHint: Disabling TLS verification allows MITM. Remove rejectUnauthorized:false / restore cert validation.

title: Authorization via tx.origin
fixHint: tx.origin auth is phishable. Use msg.sender for authorization checks.

title: On-chain pseudo-randomness from block variables
fixHint: block.* values are predictable/manipulable. Use a VRF (e.g. Chainlink) or commit-reveal for randomness.

title: Use of eval()
fixHint: Avoid eval(). Parse JSON with JSON.parse, dispatch via a lookup table, or use a proper sandbox.

title: selfdestruct present
fixHint: selfdestruct can permanently disable the contract and force-send ETH. Gate it behind strong access control or remove it.

title: Hardcoded secret assigned to a credential-named variable
fixHint: Move this value into an environment variable / secret store and load via process.env. Rotate the current value.

title: SQL built via string concatenation/interpolation
fixHint: Possible SQL injection. Use parameterized queries / prepared statements ($1, ? placeholders) instead of building the query string. If the interpolated value is a generated placeholder list (?,?,?) or a numeric coercion, this is safe — mark it with `// alltest-ignore sql-concat`.

title: Unpinned dependency: left-pad@*
fixHint: Pin left-pad to a specific version or a bounded caret/tilde range.

title: No lockfile alongside package.json
fixHint: Commit a lockfile (npm install → package-lock.json) for reproducible, auditable installs.

title: Low-level call — verify return value is checked
fixHint: Check the boolean success return of .call/.delegatecall; unchecked calls fail silently. Ensure reentrancy guards on state after external calls.

title: Powerful function — confirm access control
fixHint: Verify this state-changing/admin function has onlyOwner / role checks and (for initialize) an initializer guard.
template: function mint(address to, uint256 amount) public {

title: Powerful function — confirm access control
fixHint: Verify this state-changing/admin function has onlyOwner / role checks and (for initialize) an initializer guard.

title: Floating pragma (^) — pin the compiler
fixHint: Pin an exact compiler version (e.g. pragma solidity 0.8.24;) so deployed bytecode is reproducible.

title: No .gitignore in a code project
fixHint: Add a .gitignore appropriate to the stack (node_modules, .env, dist, etc.).

title: Lifecycle install hook present: postinstall
fixHint: Install hooks run arbitrary code on npm install. Verify it is necessary and safe.
